message:
Add ratings API, history system, and comprehensive E2E tests

New features:
- Ratings API endpoint (app/api/ratings)
- Client-side history tracking
- History store with persistence

E2E test suites:
- Comments: moderation, notifications, posting, threading
- Social: activity feed, following
- Web: language switching

Test infrastructure:
- comments-helpers.ts utilities
- social-helpers.ts utilities
- Playwright config updates

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Integrates with template renderer for variable substitution.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"","created_at":"2026-01-10T05:47:37.552140127Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-10T07:30:06.299213624Z","closed_at":"2026-01-10T07:30:06.299213624Z","close_reason":"Implemented --fill flag for interactive variables, --json output, cross-platform clipboard with graceful fallback","compaction_level":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-1hw","depends_on_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-6gx","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-10T05:49:51.272205770Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-1hw","depends_on_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-xdt","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-10T05:49:51.244455332Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"jeffreysprompts.com-1m9a","title":"Enhance copy button feedback with satisfying animation","description":"# Task: Enhance Copy Button Feedback

## Context
The copy button currently changes from Copy icon to Check icon when clicked. While functional, it lacks the "satisfying" micro-interaction that Stripe/Linear provide.

## Current Behavior
1. User clicks Copy button
2. Icon changes from Copy → Check (instant)
3. Toast appears
4. After 2s, icon changes back

## Proposed Enhancements

### 1. Icon Transition Animation
```typescript
import { motion, AnimatePresence } from 'framer-motion';

// In PromptCard.tsx copy button
<Button onClick={handleCopy} ...>
  <AnimatePresence mode="wait">
    {copied ? (
      <motion.div
        key="check"
        initial={{ scale: 0, rotate: -180 }}
        animate={{ scale: 1, rotate: 0 }}
        exit={{ scale: 0, rotate: 180 }}
        transition={{ type: "spring", stiffness: 500, damping: 25 }}
      >
        <Check className="w-4 h-4 text-emerald-500" />
      </motion.div>
    ) : (
      <motion.div
        key="copy"
        initial={{ scale: 0 }}
        animate={{ scale: 1 }}
        exit={{ scale: 0 }}
        transition={{ duration: 0.15 }}
      >
        <Copy className="w-4 h-4" />
      </motion.div>
    )}
  </AnimatePresence>
</Button>
```

### 2. Button Background Flash
```typescript
// Add success background flash
const [showFlash, setShowFlash] = useState(false);

const handleCopy = async () => {
  await navigator.clipboard.writeText(content);
  setCopied(true);
  setShowFlash(true);
  setTimeout(() => setShowFlash(false), 300);
  // ... rest of handler
};

<Button
  className={cn(
    "...",
    showFlash && "bg-emerald-100 dark:bg-emerald-900/30"
  )}
>
```

### 3. Ripple Effect (Optional)
```typescript
// CSS ripple on click
.copy-button {
  position: relative;
  overflow: hidden;
}

.copy-button::after {
  content: '';
  position: absolute;
  inset: 0;
  background: radial-gradient(circle at center, 
    oklch(0.9 0.1 145) 0%, 
    transparent 70%
  );
  opacity: 0;
  transform: scale(0);
  transition: transform 0.3s, opacity 0.3s;
}

.copy-button.copied::after {
  opacity: 1;
  transform: scale(2);
}
```

### 4. Haptic Feedback (Mobile)
```typescript
const handleCopy = async () => {
  // Haptic feedback if available
  if ('vibrate' in navigator) {
    navigator.vibrate(50); // 50ms vibration
  }
  // ... rest of copy logic
};
```

## Design Rationale
- Spring animation on Check: Feels "bouncy" and satisfying
- Rotate effect: Draws eye, makes change obvious
- Background flash: Confirms action without being distracting
- Haptic feedback: Mobile users feel the action

## Reduced Motion Support
```typescript
const prefersReducedMotion = useReducedMotion();

// Skip animation, just swap icons
{copied ? (
  <Check className="w-4 h-4 text-emerald-500" />
) : (
  <Copy className="w-4 h-4" />
)}
```

## Testing
1. Click copy - see icon spin/bounce into check
2. Background flashes briefly green
3. After 2s, icon spins back to copy
4. Mobile: feel haptic feedback
5. Reduced motion: instant icon swap
6. Multiple rapid clicks: animation doesn't break

## Files Modified
- apps/web/src/components/PromptCard.tsx
- apps/web/src/components/PromptDetailModal.tsx (same pattern)
- apps/web/src/components/mobile/SwipeablePromptCard.tsx

## Dependencies
- Framer Motion (already available)
- Lower priority than functional improvements","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"","created_at":"2026-01-12T06:09:39.419096088Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-12T15:16:43.728049147Z","closed_at":"2026-01-12T15:16:43.728049147Z","close_reason":"Implemented satisfying micro-interactions with spring animations, background flash, and haptic feedback","compaction_level":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-1m9a","depends_on_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-f3zc","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-12T06:24:56.158090338Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"jeffreysprompts.com-1nvi","title":"E2E Tests: Roadmap & Voting","description":"## Origin
Moved from jeffreysprompts_premium jeffreysprompts_premium-1jon.

## Task
Create E2E tests for public roadmap and feature voting.

## Why This Matters
- Ensures community engagement features work
- Prevents abuse of voting system

## Test Scenarios
1. View public roadmap list
2. Submit a vote and see count update
3. Prevent duplicate votes from same user
4. Admin hides a roadmap item

## Logging Requirements
- Log vote actions and response codes
- Screenshot roadmap list and vote state

## Files to Create
- e2e/roadmap/roadmap.spec.ts

## Success Criteria
- Voting works as expected
- Duplicate votes prevented
- Admin controls effective

-{"id":"jeffreysprompts.com-1qt5","title":"EPIC: Sharing & Collaboration","description":"## Origin
Moved from jeffreysprompts_premium jeffreysprompts_premium-oam.

## Overview
Sharing enables users to distribute their prompts, packs, and collections with others - either publicly via the Swap Meet or privately via direct links.

## Sharing Modes

### 1. Public Sharing (Swap Meet)
- Make content visible to all premium users
- Appears in community browse/search
- Receives ratings and usage stats
- Builds author reputation

### 2. Private Link Sharing
- Generate unique shareable URL
- Anyone with link can view (no auth required for viewing)
- Track link views/uses
- Revoke link anytime
- Optional: password protection
- Optional: expiration date

### 3. Team/Workspace Sharing (V2)
- Share with specific team members
- Workspace-level shared library
- Role-based permissions (view/edit/admin)
- Activity feed for shared content

## Key Features

### Share Dialog
- Choose sharing mode
- Generate/copy share link
- Set link options (password, expiry)
- Preview shared view
- Social share buttons (Twitter, LinkedIn)

### Manage Shared Content
- My Shared Items dashboard
- View share statistics
- Edit sharing settings
- Revoke access
- See who accessed (for team shares)

### Receive Shared Content
- View shared content via link
- Save to own library (if premium)
- Fork/duplicate to own account
- Rate and review (if public)

### Share Targets
- Individual prompts
- Collections
- Prompt packs
- Skills

## Database Schema
- share_links (id, user_id, content_type, content_id, link_code, password_hash, expires_at, created_at)
- share_link_views (link_id, viewer_id, viewed_at, ip_hash)
- team_shares (id, content_type, content_id, team_id, permission_level, shared_by, shared_at)

## Security Considerations
- Rate limit link generation
- Obfuscated link codes (not sequential)
- Optional password protection
- View logging for audit
- DMCA/abuse reporting for public shares
","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","assignee":"SilverPine","created_at":"2026-01-11T06:22:54.220011923Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-21T09:08:20.730582386Z","compaction_level":0,"comments":[{"id":7,"issue_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-1qt5","author":"SilverPine","text":"## Progress Update

### Completed Work
1. **Wired /share/[linkCode] page to real API** - Replaced mock data with fetch calls to /api/share/[code] and /api/share/[code]/verify. Handles password protection, expiration, and 404/410 states.

2. **Wired ShareDialog to real API** - handleCreateShare() now calls POST /api/share, handleRevokeShare() calls DELETE /api/share/[code]. Removed mock code.

3. **Created /settings/shared page** - New 'My Shared Links' page using ShareManagement component.

4. **Added Settings page link** - Added 'My Shared Links' card to /settings under new 'Sharing' section.

### Existing Infrastructure Found
- ShareDialog.tsx, ShareManagement.tsx, share-link-store.ts (in-memory)
- API endpoints: /api/share/*, /api/share/mine

### Remaining Work
- Database persistence (currently in-memory - data lost on restart)
- Fork/duplicate backend implementation
- Team/Workspace sharing (V2)","created_at":"2026-01-21T09:02:15Z"},{"id":8,"issue_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-1qt5","author":"SilverPine","text":"## Additional Analysis

### Database Persistence
The codebase uses in-memory stores (globalThis Maps) for ALL features - ratings, reports, moderation, tickets, profiles, etc. Adding database persistence for share links would require setting up database infrastructure for the entire project. This is blocked until a broader database initiative is undertaken.

### Fork/Duplicate Feature
The share page has UI buttons for 'Save to Library' and 'Fork' but they are placeholder implementations (just show toasts). Real implementation requires:
1. Authentication system (not yet implemented)
2. User library/saved prompts storage (not yet implemented)

### Current Status Summary
- **COMPLETE**: All UI wiring to real API endpoints
- **COMPLETE**: My Shared Links management page
- **BLOCKED**: Database persistence (needs project-wide DB setup)
- **BLOCKED**: Fork/duplicate (needs auth system)
- **FUTURE (V2)**: Team/Workspace sharing

The sharing feature is functionally complete within the current in-memory architecture constraints.","created_at":"2026-01-21T09:08:20Z"}]}

Optional analytics tracking: prompt_view, prompt_copy, search (query length + result count, emit zero_results when count==0, optionally hash query), export, skill_install, workflow_copy. Use Plausible or similar privacy-respecting service. No PII collection. -{"id":"jeffreysprompts.com-1w92","title":"E2E Tests: Following and Activity Feed","description":"## Origin
Moved from jeffreysprompts_premium jeffreysprompts_premium-ypuk.

## Task
Create comprehensive E2E tests for the following system and activity feed.

## Test Scenarios
1. **Following Users**
   - Follow button works
   - Unfollow works
   - Following count updates
   - Followers count updates

2. **Activity Feed**
   - Feed shows followed users' activity
   - New posts appear in real-time (or on refresh)
   - Empty feed message
   - Load more pagination

3. **Activity Types**
   - New prompt published
   - Prompt updated
   - Collection shared
   - User joined (optional)

4. **Filtering**
   - Filter by activity type
   - Date range filter
   - Hide own activity option

5. **Notifications**
   - New follower notification
   - Can toggle follow notifications

## Logging Requirements
- Log follow/unfollow actions
- Log feed loads and pagination
- Log filter changes
- Screenshot feed states

## Files to Create
- e2e/social/following.spec.ts
- e2e/social/activity-feed.spec.ts
- e2e/lib/social-helpers.ts

## Success Criteria
- Following system works reliably
- Feed shows correct content
- Real-time or timely updates
- Good empty states

@@ -135,7 +135,7 @@
 {"id":"jeffreysprompts.com-9r7s","title":"Add --json flag support for all new CLI commands","description":"Add --json flag support to all new CLI commands for machine-readable output.

Commands to Update:
- jfp login (output: { authenticated: true, email, tier })
- jfp logout (output: { logged_out: true })
- jfp whoami (output: { email, tier, user_id })
- jfp collections list (output: { collections: [...] })
- jfp collections show (output: { collection, items: [...] })
- jfp notes (output: { prompt_id, notes: [...] })
- jfp save (output: { saved: true, prompt_id })
- jfp list --mine/--saved (output: { prompts: [...] })

Implementation:
- Add --json/-j flag to all relevant commands
- When --json is set, output only valid JSON to stdout
- Errors should also be JSON: { error: true, message, code }
- Progress/status messages go to stderr when --json is enabled
- Ensure consistent field naming across all commands

 {"id":"jeffreysprompts.com-9rgl","title":"Redesign 'Clear All Filters' as a proper button","description":"# Task: Redesign Clear Filters Button

## Current Implementation (page.tsx:165-172)
```tsx
{hasActiveFilters && (
  <button
    onClick={clearFilters}
    className="text-sm text-indigo-600 hover:text-indigo-800 dark:text-indigo-400 dark:hover:text-indigo-300 transition-colors"
  >
    Clear all filters
  </button>
)}
```

## Problems
1. Plain text link - low visual prominence
2. Uses indigo (inconsistent with neutral design system)
3. Only visible when filters active (no affordance)
4. No icon - relies entirely on text

## Proposed Design

### Option A: Always-visible ghost button (Recommended)
```tsx
<Button
  variant="ghost"
  size="sm"
  onClick={clearFilters}
  disabled={!hasActiveFilters}
  className={cn(
    "h-8 px-3 text-sm font-medium transition-colors",
    hasActiveFilters
      ? "text-neutral-700 dark:text-neutral-300 hover:text-neutral-900 dark:hover:text-white"
      : "text-neutral-400 dark:text-neutral-600 cursor-not-allowed opacity-50"
  )}
>
  <X className="w-3.5 h-3.5 mr-1.5" />
  Clear filters
  {hasActiveFilters && (
    <span className="ml-1.5 text-xs bg-neutral-200 dark:bg-neutral-700 px-1.5 py-0.5 rounded-full">
      {filterCount}
    </span>
  )}
</Button>
```

### Option B: Only when active, but as a proper button
```tsx
{hasActiveFilters && (
  <Button
    variant="outline"
    size="sm"
    onClick={clearFilters}
    className="h-8 px-3 text-sm gap-1.5"
  >
    <X className="w-3.5 h-3.5" />
    Clear ({filterCount})
  </Button>
)}
```

## Implementation Details

### Add filter count calculation
```tsx
const filterCount = useMemo(() => {
  let count = 0;
  if (filters.query) count++;
  if (filters.category) count++;
  count += filters.tags.length;
  return count;
}, [filters]);
```

### Add accessibility
```tsx
aria-label={`Clear all ${filterCount} active filters`}
```

## Design Rationale
- Ghost button maintains visual hierarchy (filters are secondary to results)
- Always visible = discoverable (disabled state indicates "nothing to clear")
- Icon + text = clear meaning
- Count badge = precise feedback
- Matches CategoryFilter/TagFilter clear button pattern

## Testing
1. With no filters: button disabled, grayed out
2. With 1 filter: button enabled, shows "(1)"
3. With multiple: button shows count
4. Click clears all and disables button
5. Keyboard accessible (Tab, Enter)

## Files Modified
- apps/web/src/app/page.tsx

## Dependencies
- Should use the updated neutral color palette (do after color fix)
- Matches pattern already used in TagFilter.tsx (Clear button)","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"","created_at":"2026-01-12T05:38:18.479565228Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-12T15:06:14.296451909Z","closed_at":"2026-01-12T15:06:14.296451909Z","close_reason":"Implementation verified complete: Button component with ghost variant, X icon, filterCount memo, aria-label, and neutral colors. Build passes.","compaction_level":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-9rgl","depends_on_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-ogel","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-12T05:43:15.125413399Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"","thread_id":""}]}

-{"id":"jeffreysprompts.com-9wk9","title":"EPIC: Ratings & Reviews System","description":"## Origin
Moved from jeffreysprompts_premium jeffreysprompts_premium-cdp.

## Overview
Ratings and reviews provide quality signals for community content, helping users discover the best prompts, packs, and skills while rewarding creators for quality contributions.

## Rating System Design

### Simple Rating (MVP)
- Thumbs up / thumbs down (binary)
- Easy, low-friction interaction
- Clear positive/negative signal
- Calculate "approval rate" percentage

### Extended Rating (V2)
- 1-5 star rating
- Written review (optional)
- Helpful/not helpful on reviews
- Response from author

## Key Features

### Rate Content
- One-click rating on any ratable content
- Rate: prompts, packs, skills
- Change rating anytime
- See your rating history

### View Ratings
- Show rating on content cards
- Rating breakdown on detail pages
- Sort by rating in lists
- Filter by minimum rating

### Reviews (V2)
- Write text review with rating
- Edit/delete own reviews
- Report inappropriate reviews
- Author can respond to reviews

### Leaderboards
- Top rated prompts this week/month
- Top contributors by ratings received
- Rising stars (new content with high ratings)

## Anti-Gaming Measures
- One rating per user per item
- Rate limiting on rapid ratings
- Flag suspicious patterns
- Weight by user reputation

## Database Schema
- ratings (id, user_id, content_type, content_id, rating_value, created_at, updated_at)
- reviews (id, user_id, content_type, content_id, rating_id, content, created_at)
- review_votes (review_id, user_id, is_helpful)

## UI Components
- RatingButton (thumbs up/down)
- RatingDisplay (show current rating)
- RatingBreakdown (detailed stats)
- ReviewList (paginated reviews)
- ReviewForm (write review)

## Integration Points
- Community prompts (Swap Meet)
- Prompt packs
- Skills
- Collections (V2)

 {"id":"jeffreysprompts.com-cwcj","title":"Add share_links schema tables","description":"## Origin
Moved from jeffreysprompts_premium jeffreysprompts_premium-318.

## Task
Add share_links table for private link sharing of content.

## Schema Definition
```typescript
export const shareLinks = pgTable("share_links", {
  id: uuid("id").primaryKey().defaultRandom(),
  userId: uuid("user_id").notNull().references(() => users.id),
  
  // What is being shared
  contentType: text("content_type", { 
    enum: ["user_prompt", "prompt_pack", "skill", "collection"] 
  }).notNull(),
  contentId: uuid("content_id").notNull(),
  
  // The shareable link code (appears in URL)
  linkCode: text("link_code").notNull().unique(),
  
  // Optional security
  passwordHash: text("password_hash"),  // bcrypt hash if password protected
  
  // Expiration
  expiresAt: timestamp("expires_at", { withTimezone: true }),
  
  // Stats
  viewCount: integer("view_count").default(0),
  
  // Status
  isActive: boolean("is_active").default(true).notNull(),
  
  // Timestamps
  createdAt: timestamp("created_at", { withTimezone: true }).defaultNow().notNull(),
}, (table) => [
  index("idx_share_links_user").on(table.userId),
  index("idx_share_links_code").on(table.linkCode),
  index("idx_share_links_content").on(table.contentType, table.contentId),
]);

export const shareLinkViews = pgTable("share_link_views", {
  id: uuid("id").primaryKey().defaultRandom(),
  linkId: uuid("link_id").notNull().references(() => shareLinks.id, { onDelete: "cascade" }),
  
  // Viewer info (optional - null for anonymous)
  viewerId: uuid("viewer_id").references(() => users.id),
  
  // For analytics without PII
  ipHash: text("ip_hash"),  // SHA-256 of IP for unique visitor counting
  userAgent: text("user_agent"),
  
  viewedAt: timestamp("viewed_at", { withTimezone: true }).defaultNow().notNull(),
}, (table) => [
  index("idx_share_link_views_link").on(table.linkId),
]);
```

## Link Code Generation
- 12 character alphanumeric (base62)
- Example: "x7KmN2pQ4rYz"
- Not sequential/guessable
- Check for collisions on insert

## Public URLs
- /share/{linkCode} - view shared content
- No auth required to view
- Optional password prompt

## Notes
- viewCount is denormalized; increment on each view
- isActive allows "revoking" without deleting
- ipHash allows counting unique visitors without storing IPs

-{"id":"jeffreysprompts.com-d4t1","title":"Implement recently viewed history","description":"## Origin
Moved from jeffreysprompts_premium jeffreysprompts_premium-7wcm.

## Task
Track and display user's recently viewed prompts/content for easy access.

## Why This Matters
- Quick access to recently browsed content
- Find that prompt you saw earlier
- Reduce re-search friction
- Personalization signal

## Features

### 1. Tracking
- Track prompt views
- Track collection views
- Track skill views
- Track search queries

### 2. History Display
- Recent items in sidebar
- Full history page
- Searchable history
- Clear history option

### 3. Privacy
- Only track for logged-in users
- Clear on request
- Auto-expire after 30 days
- Not shared with others

## Database Schema
```typescript
export const viewHistory = pgTable('view_history', {
  id: uuid('id').primaryKey().defaultRandom(),
  userId: uuid('user_id').references(() => userProfiles.id).notNull(),
  
  // What was viewed
  resourceType: text('resource_type').notNull(), // prompt, collection, skill, pack
  resourceId: uuid('resource_id').notNull(),
  
  // Context
  source: text('source'), // search, browse, direct, recommendation
  searchQuery: text('search_query'), // if from search
  
  // Timestamps
  viewedAt: timestamp('viewed_at').defaultNow(),
  duration: integer('duration'), // seconds on page (optional)
}, (table) => [
  index('idx_history_user').on(table.userId),
  index('idx_history_resource').on(table.resourceType, table.resourceId),
  index('idx_history_time').on(table.viewedAt),
]);
```

## Implementation

### Track View
```typescript
async function trackView(userId: string, resource: ViewResource) {
  // Dedupe rapid views (ignore if viewed in last 5 min)
  const recent = await getRecentView(userId, resource.id);
  if (recent && isRecent(recent.viewedAt, 5 * 60 * 1000)) {
    return; // Skip duplicate
  }
  
  await db.insert(viewHistory).values({
    userId,
    resourceType: resource.type,
    resourceId: resource.id,
    source: resource.source,
  });
}
```

### Get History
```typescript
async function getRecentlyViewed(userId: string, limit = 10) {
  return db.select()
    .from(viewHistory)
    .where(eq(viewHistory.userId, userId))
    .orderBy(desc(viewHistory.viewedAt))
    .limit(limit);
}
```

## UI Components
- RecentlyViewedSidebar - Quick access list
- HistoryPage - Full browsable history
- ClearHistoryButton - Privacy control

## Files to Create
- src/lib/db/schema/history.ts
- src/lib/history/tracking.ts
- src/app/api/history/route.ts
- src/app/(app)/history/page.tsx
- src/components/history/recently-viewed.tsx

## Success Criteria
- Views tracked accurately
- Deduplication works
- History accessible
- Privacy controls work

 {"id":"jeffreysprompts.com-dc1k","title":"Implement comments and discussions on prompts","description":"## Origin
Moved from jeffreysprompts_premium jeffreysprompts_premium-4aeq.

## Task
Allow users to comment on and discuss public prompts.

## Why This Matters
- Community engagement
- Feedback for creators
- Knowledge sharing
- Questions and answers

## Features

### 1. Comment System
- Add comments to public prompts
- Reply to comments (threaded)
- Edit own comments
- Delete own comments
- Report inappropriate comments

### 2. Discussion Features
- Upvote helpful comments
- Pin important comments (creator only)
- Sort by newest/oldest/popular
- Collapse/expand threads
- Load more pagination

### 3. Notifications
- Notify creator of new comments
- Notify on replies to your comments
- Notify on mentions (@username)
- Email digest option

### 4. Moderation
- Creator can hide comments
- Creator can disable comments
- Admin can remove comments
- Spam detection

## Database Schema
```typescript
export const promptComments = pgTable('prompt_comments', {
  id: uuid('id').primaryKey().defaultRandom(),
  promptId: uuid('prompt_id').references(() => userPrompts.id).notNull(),
  authorId: uuid('author_id').references(() => userProfiles.id).notNull(),
  
  // Content
  content: text('content').notNull(),
  
  // Threading
  parentId: uuid('parent_id').references(() => promptComments.id),
  depth: integer('depth').default(0),
  
  // Status
  isHidden: boolean('is_hidden').default(false),
  isPinned: boolean('is_pinned').default(false),
  
  // Engagement
  upvoteCount: integer('upvote_count').default(0),
  replyCount: integer('reply_count').default(0),
  
  // Timestamps
  createdAt: timestamp('created_at').defaultNow(),
  editedAt: timestamp('edited_at'),
}, (table) => [
  index('idx_comments_prompt').on(table.promptId),
  index('idx_comments_author').on(table.authorId),
  index('idx_comments_parent').on(table.parentId),
]);

export const commentUpvotes = pgTable('comment_upvotes', {
  commentId: uuid('comment_id').references(() => promptComments.id).notNull(),
  userId: uuid('user_id').references(() => userProfiles.id).notNull(),
  createdAt: timestamp('created_at').defaultNow(),
}, (table) => [
  primaryKey({ columns: [table.commentId, table.userId] }),
]);
```

## API Endpoints
- GET /api/prompts/[id]/comments
- POST /api/prompts/[id]/comments
- PATCH /api/comments/[id]
- DELETE /api/comments/[id]
- POST /api/comments/[id]/upvote
- POST /api/comments/[id]/report

## UI Components
- CommentSection - Full comment display
- CommentThread - Single thread
- CommentForm - New comment input
- CommentItem - Individual comment
- ReplyButton, UpvoteButton

## Limits
- Max 5000 characters per comment
- Max 10 levels of nesting
- Rate limit: 10 comments/hour
- Premium: higher limits

## Files to Create
- src/lib/db/schema/comments.ts
- src/app/api/prompts/[id]/comments/route.ts
- src/app/api/comments/[id]/route.ts
- src/components/comments/
- src/lib/comments/moderation.ts

## Success Criteria
- Comments work smoothly
- Threading displays correctly
- Notifications sent
- Moderation tools work

 {"id":"jeffreysprompts.com-del9","title":"E2E Tests: CLI Authentication Flow","description":"Create comprehensive E2E tests for CLI authentication covering both local browser and remote device code flows.

Test Scenarios:
- Local browser flow: happy path, port conflicts, timeout, browser launch failure
- Remote device code flow: happy path, code expiration, slow poll, denied, invalid code
- Token persistence: save/load credentials, auto-refresh, re-login on expiry
- Integration tests: full login->whoami->list->logout flow

Logging Requirements:
- Log each test step with timestamps
- Log HTTP requests/responses (sanitize tokens)
- Log credential file operations
- Log browser/process spawning events
- Dump full state on failure for debugging

Test Infrastructure:
- Use Bun test runner
- Mock HTTP server for premium site endpoints
- Temporary credential directory per test
- Cleanup hooks for all resources
